name: Dinks & Drives
about: Dinks & Drives is an indoor venue in Melbourne, FL, featuring a combination of high-end golf simulators and state-of-the-art indoor pickleball courts. The facility offers flexible membership packages and hourly bookings for both sports, targeting players of all skill levels. It is equipped with advanced golf simulator technology providing real-time swing analysis and access to world-renowned golf courses, along with premium pickleball courts designed for competitive and recreational play. The venue also functions as a social lounge, catering to individuals looking to perfect their swings, sharpen their serves, or unwind in a vibrant community setting.
why_golfers_like_it: State-of-the-art golf simulator technology with precision tracking
Access to iconic golf courses from around the world
Multiple themed simulator rooms (King Room, Big Cat Room, Golden Bear Room)
Flexible membership options and hourly booking
On-site coaching available for skill refinement

food_and_drink.notes: No mention of on-site food service; BYOB likely allowed
No explicit mention of alcohol service; venue type suggests BYOB policy

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The website does not explicitly state whether equipment rentals are included with memberships or hourly bookings. However, it mentions 'Equipment Rentals' under 'New Equipment,' suggesting clubs may be available for rent.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: The venue operates as a social lounge and does not mention an on-site bar or kitchen, strongly suggesting a BYOB (bring your own beverages) policy is permitted.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: While the exact time for 18 holes is not specified, golf simulators typically allow for faster rounds than traditional golf, often completing 18 holes in 1-2 hours depending on play style and simulator software.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the venue is described as being for 'players of all levels,' and the inclusion of family-oriented services and events suggests it welcomes families and children.
